items.title: Government keeps life-changing MS medicines on PBS amid drug pricing row

children.content: Health Minister Mark Butler has accepted independent advice for a rapid review of multiple sclerosis (MS) drugs to ensure they remain available on the PBS.

gallery.altText: A pile of drugs on a table, all are different shapes and sizes.

thumbnail.altText: A pile of drugs on a table, all are different shapes and sizes.

items.title: Charges expected to be laid over Laos methanol poisoning deaths

children.content: A Melbourne father whose daughter died from methanol poisoning in Laos has said he is "disgusted" over the charges expected to be laid.

image.altText: Side-by-side photos of Bianca Jones and Holly Bowles, two Melbourne teens who have been hospitalised in Thailand

items.title: Sydney home ownership rate hits 70-year low

children.content: Sydney is no longer affordable for most, as new data reveals the number of people who own the home they live in is the lowest it has been in decades.

image.altText: Terraces on street with cars and gates visible

items.title: 'Haven't had a day off all year': PM defends broken festival promise

children.content: A year ago the Anthony Albanese vowed to attend the Garma Festival every year for as long as he remained prime minister. Now he says he needs a break.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese, wearing a hat and no tie, addresses a crowd.

items.title: Major disturbance unfolds at Perth's maximum-security prison

children.content: A prison officer has been injured in a major disturbance that caused significant damage at Perth’s maximum-security prison.

image.altText: The front façade of a prison at dusk

items.title: Transgender woman's 'humiliating' experience with major corporations

children.content: What should have been a quick process of updating personal details left Laura Mitchell "insulted" and "humiliated" by the country's largest institutions.

gallery.altText: Woman with brown hair looks out at the water while standing at Frankston Pier.

thumbnail.altText: Woman with brown hair looks out at the water while standing at Frankston Pier.

items.title: BHP staff down tools for historic strike in Western Australia

image.altText: Workers on strike out side of BHP's Port Hedland facilities.

children.content: Workers at the mining giant's operations in Port Hedland have walked off the job in one of the most significant industrial disputes to hit the resources sector in a quarter of a century.

items.title: Terrorists using AI to build weapons and plan attacks, report finds

children.content: A report finds AI systems can be tricked into coaching extremists how to make bombs and plan attacks.

image.altText: Phone screen with a collection of AI apps on it, including Claude and ChatGPT.

items.title: Albanese announces AI plan, Hanson joins Rinehart in Sicily

children.content: It might not have been a sitting week but it has been a busy few days in politics with the prime minister's AI announcement, a raft of politicians inking book deals and a spotting of Pauline Hanson and Gina Rinehart in Sicily.

image.altText: AlbaneseUYSYDwrap

items.title: NZ's South Island struck by magnitude-5.9 earthquake

children.content: The earthquake struck 40 kilometres north of Te Anau, with the west coast of the South Island from Milford Sound to Puysegur Point, likely to be affected.

image.altText: A map of an earthquake.

items.title: Stink bomb attack planners taken to court by energy giant Woodside

children.content: Energy giant Woodside has taken climate activists to court in a bid to unmask the conspirators behind a stink-bomb protest that forced the evacuation of its Perth headquarters.

image.altText: Kristen and Joana stand side by side outside court while looking straight at the camera

items.title: ANU head apologises for failing to protect Jewish staff, students

children.content: The interim vice-chancellor of the Australian National University has apologised for the institution's failure to protect the psychological safety of Jewish students and staff during pro-Palestinian encampments in 2024.

gallery.altText: A woman with long blonde hair wearing a black blazer stands in a conference room looking serious.

thumbnail.altText: A woman with long blonde hair wearing a black blazer stands in a conference room looking serious.

items.title: Geelong win over Saints soured by injury to star Holmes

children.content: Geelong beats St Kilda at Kardinia Park to kick off round 19 of the AFL, but the injury list gets longer with a concerning leg issue for Max Holmes. Follow live.

image.altText: A Geelong AFL player grimaces as he is helped around the boundary by medical staff after being injured.

items.title: 'Reece Walsh was unbelievable': Broncos keep season alive with upset win

children.content: Brisbane fullback Reece Walsh has been described as "unbelievable" by Penrith's coach after leading the Broncos to an upset win on Thursday, ending an eight-match losing streak and keeping their slim finals chances alive.

image.altText: Reece Walsh of the Broncos runs with the ball

items.title: New monkey species with orange lips discovered after years of searching

children.content: The monkey —
colobus congoensis —
is only the fifth new species of African monkey to be discovered in the past 75 years.

items.title: The Odyssey may be the epic we deserve, but not the one we need

children.content: The scope of Christopher Nolan's latest epic might be awesome but a menagerie of supporting faces and questionable characterisations muddle the director's vision.

image.altText: A man dressed as a solider stands in front of a crowd and ships.

items.title: I thought I could use voodoo to win a TV competition, instead I won back my joy

children.content: Halfway through the Race Around The World competition, I'm second last on the leaderboard. So, I decide to cheat.

image.altText: A voodoo doll of Kate.

items.title: Why Australians shouldn't panic about fuel prices as war in Iran escalates again

children.content: With the price of oil rising and the fuel excise cut due to end, drivers are facing twin threats when it comes to the price of fuel, but experts say Australia is in a much better position compared with when the Middle East conflict began.

articles.title: Iran frees US citizen after almost two years of imprisonment

items.title: NT government slammed over no-show to federal racism inquiry

children.content: A national inquiry into racism against First Nations people, launched after an alleged terror attack in Perth, has heard allegations of systemic racism in the Northern Territory, as the territory government comes under fire for snubbing the probe.

items.title: Delay in revealing theft of medical data labelled 'unacceptable'

children.content: Cybersecurity experts have questioned why Partnered Health took more than three weeks to reveal it had been the victim of a data breach.

image.altText: A close-up shot of a man's hands typing on a keyboard in a dark room.

items.title: Drug traffickers jailed over failed plot to smuggle cocaine from PNG by plane

children.content: Two masterminds behind a 2020 plot to bring $80 million worth of cocaine into Australia were sentenced in Victoria’s County Court, five years after a failed drug trafficking flight.

items.title: Ex-prisoners say Australia should do more for citizens detained overseas

children.content: Australians who have endured prolonged detention overseas call on the government to adopt a more strategic approach to cases involving citizens, especially those held by countries that practice "hostage diplomacy".

image.altText: A man in a white singlet takes a selfie with a smiling woman with her head on his shoulder.

items.title: Sam Neill's cause of death revealed by agent

children.content: New Zealand actor Sam Neill died from pneumonia, his long-time agent Philip Grenz has confirmed.

image.altText: Sam Neill wears a scarf around his neck and jacket. Behind are black and white images of him over the years

items.title: You'll now be able to spot an AI-generated song on Spotify

children.content: Streaming giant Spotify has stopped short of expunging artificially generated music from its platform, but it is making it easier for listeners to work out that they are listening to tracks produced using AI.

articles.title: Community meets over plans for large 'AI factory' at River Murray town

children.content: An AI company says its proposed projects have the potential to create hundreds of jobs in South Australia, but residents of one River Murray town say they want to know more about the potential impacts.

image.altText: Tailem Bend locals at a public forum.

articles.title: Albanese rolls out rule book and red carpet for AI companies

children.content: If Australia is truly going to coax major AI companies to choose us rather than the other suitor nations with similar ambitions, some tough decisions need to be made soon.

articles.title: Artists seek ‘seat at table’ in AI office

children.content: The creative sector hopes there is a space to contribute to the Office of AI, given its content powers a lot of these tools.

items.title: Moira Deeming to be disendorsed as a Liberal candidate after dropping court action

children.content: Victoria's Liberal Party is set to drop Moira Deeming as an upper house candidate following contested claims against former state leader Matthew Guy.

items.title: Federal MP and staffer injured in boating accident

children.content: Tasmanian police say a man fell from an oyster barge when it hit a sandbar and was briefly trapped. Member for Barker Tony Pasin says he and a staffer were injured and now receiving medical treatment, but it is not clear who fell from the barge.

image.altText: Exteriors of a public hospital.

items.title: Starmer says political journey 'over' as he faces last question session

children.content: Outgoing UK Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer, who announced his resignation last month, has taken part in his last weekly grilling from MPs in parliament.

items.title: Activists fear China's ethnic unity law could silence critics in Australia

children.content: Thirty-nine community groups in Australia and around the world issue a statement rejecting and strongly condemning China's ethnic unity law.

items.title: Alleged 'fight club' organiser arrested, SA Police say

children.content: SA Police have arrested the alleged organiser of a 'fight club' and reported seven other teenagers they allege were linked to videos of amateur bouts that were posted online.

image.altText: The South Australia Police logo on a building.

items.title: Death-defying Argentina living every World Cup game as if it's the last

children.content: Argentina has been the daredevils of this World Cup, and after another incredible comeback to defeat England in the semifinals, its heady mix of emotion and skill, might just deliver back-to-back titles.

articles.title: England's hopes fade again, but it won't stop them dreaming

children.content: England aren't going to be bringing the World Cup home, 60 years of hurt due to extend at least another four years after the devastation of another semifinal defeat.

items.title: Cyclist bitten by brown snake caught in her bike chain

children.content: The woman, in her 60s, was riding on a northern NSW rail trail when she ran over a two-metre snake that became trapped then bit her.
